sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.35 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: (R.\,Bieri, R.\,Strebel). Let${ o}$be an associative ring with identity distinct from zero. A group$G$is said to be almost finitely presented over${ o}$if it has a presentation$G=F/R$where$F$is a finitely generated free group and the${ o}G$-module$R/[R,R] _{{ Z}} { o}$is finitely generated. It is easy to see that every finitely presented group$G$is almost finitely presented over${ Z}$and therefore...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
